# pathlib-abc

{{ pypi }} {{ docs }}

Base classes for `pathlib.Path`-ish objects. Requires Python 3.7+.

This package is a preview of `pathlib` functionality planned for a future
release of Python; specifically, it provides two ABCs that can be used to
implement path classes for non-local filesystems, such as archive files and
storage servers:

`PurePathBase`

: Abstract base class for paths that do not perform I/O.

`PathBase`

: Abstract base class for paths that perform I/O.

These base classes are under active development. Once the base classes reach
maturity, they will be made part of the Python standard library, and this
package will continue to provide a backport for older Python releases.

## Contributing

Changes to `pathlib_abc.py` and `test_pathlib_abc.py` must be made in the
upstream CPython project, and undergo their usual CLA + code review process.
Once a change lands in CPython, it can be back-ported here.

Other changes (such as CI improvements) can be made as pull requests to this
project.

functions.name: pathlib_abc._glob.translate
documentation:
Translate a pathname with shell wildcards to a regular expression.

If `recursive` is true, the pattern segment '**' will match any number of
path segments.

If `include_hidden` is true, wildcards can match path segments beginning
with a dot ('.').

If a sequence of separator characters is given to `seps`, they will be
used to split the pattern into segments and match path separators. If not
given, os.path.sep and os.path.altsep (where available) are used.

functions.name: pathlib_abc._glob._compile_pattern
documentation:
Compile given glob pattern to a re.Pattern object (observing case
sensitivity).

classes.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.lexists
documentation: Implements os.path.lexists().
Abstract methods

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.scandir
documentation: Implements os.scandir().

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.add_slash
documentation: Returns a path with a trailing slash added.

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.concat_path
documentation: Implements path concatenation.

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.parse_entry
documentation: Returns the path of an entry yielded from scandir().

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.compile
documentation: High-level methods

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.selector
documentation:
Returns a function that selects from a given path, walking and
filtering according to the glob-style pattern parts in *parts*.

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.special_selector
documentation: Returns a function that selects special children of the given path.

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.literal_selector
documentation: Returns a function that selects a literal descendant of a path.

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.wildcard_selector
documentation:
Returns a function that selects direct children of a given path,
filtering by pattern.

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.recursive_selector
documentation:
Returns a function that selects a given path and all its children,
recursively, filtering by pattern.

methods.name: pathlib_abc._glob._GlobberBase.select_exists
documentation: Yields the given path, if it exists.
Abstract class providing shell-style pattern matching and globbing.

classes.name: pathlib_abc._glob._StringGlobber
documentation: Provides shell-style pattern matching and globbing for string paths.

modules.name: pathlib_abc
documentation:
Abstract base classes for rich path objects.

This module is published as a PyPI package called "pathlib-abc".

This module is also a *PRIVATE* part of the Python standard library, where
it's developed alongside pathlib. If it finds success and maturity as a PyPI
package, it could become a public part of the standard library.

Two base classes are defined here -- PurePathBase and PathBase -- that
resemble pathlib's PurePath and Path respectively.

classes.name: pathlib_abc.UnsupportedOperation
documentation:
An exception that is raised when an unsupported operation is called on
a path object.

classes.name: pathlib_abc.ParserBase

methods.name: pathlib_abc.ParserBase.sep
documentation: The character used to separate path components.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.ParserBase.join
documentation: Join path segments.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.ParserBase.split
documentation:
Split the path into a pair (head, tail), where *head* is everything
before the final path separator, and *tail* is everything after.
Either part may be empty.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.ParserBase.splitdrive
documentation:
Split the path into a 2-item tuple (drive, tail), where *drive* is
a device name or mount point, and *tail* is everything after the
drive. Either part may be empty.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.ParserBase.splitext
documentation:
Split the path into a pair (root, ext), where *ext* is empty or
begins with a begins with a period and contains at most one period,
and *root* is everything before the extension.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.ParserBase.normcase
documentation: Normalize the case of the path.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.ParserBase.isabs
documentation:
Returns whether the path is absolute, i.e. unaffected by the
current directory or drive.

Base class for path parsers, which do low-level path manipulation.

Path parsers provide a subset of the os.path API, specifically those
functions needed to provide PurePathBase functionality. Each PurePathBase
subclass references its path parser via a 'parser' class attribute.

Every method in this base class raises an UnsupportedOperation exception.

classes.name: pathlib_abc.PathGlobber

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathGlobber.scandir
documentation:
Emulates os.scandir(), which returns an object that can be used as
a context manager. This method is called by walk() and glob().

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathGlobber.concat_path
documentation: Appends text to the given path.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathGlobber.parse_entry
documentation: Returns the path of an entry yielded from scandir().
Class providing shell-style globbing for path objects.

classes.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.with_segments
documentation:
Construct a new path object from any number of path-like objects.
Subclasses may override this method to customize how new path objects
are created from methods like `iterdir()`.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.__str__
documentation:
Return the string representation of the path, suitable for
passing to system calls.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.as_posix
documentation:
Return the string representation of the path with forward (/)
slashes.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.drive
documentation: The drive prefix (letter or UNC path), if any.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.root
documentation: The root of the path, if any.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.anchor
documentation: The concatenation of the drive and root, or ''.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.name
documentation: The final path component, if any.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.suffix
documentation:
The final component's last suffix, if any.

This includes the leading period. For example: '.txt'

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.suffixes
documentation:
A list of the final component's suffixes, if any.

These include the leading periods. For example: ['.tar', '.gz']

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.stem
documentation: The final path component, minus its last suffix.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.with_name
documentation: Return a new path with the file name changed.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.with_stem
documentation: Return a new path with the stem changed.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.with_suffix
documentation:
Return a new path with the file suffix changed.  If the path
has no suffix, add given suffix.  If the given suffix is an empty
string, remove the suffix from the path.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.relative_to
documentation:
Return the relative path to another path identified by the passed
arguments.  If the operation is not possible (because this is not
related to the other path), raise ValueError.

The *walk_up* parameter controls whether `..` may be used to resolve
the path.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.is_relative_to
documentation: Return True if the path is relative to another path or False.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.parts
documentation:
An object providing sequence-like access to the
components in the filesystem path.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.joinpath
documentation:
Combine this path with one or several arguments, and return a
new path representing either a subpath (if all arguments are relative
paths) or a totally different path (if one of the arguments is
anchored).

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase._stack
documentation:
Split the path into a 2-tuple (anchor, parts), where *anchor* is the
uppermost parent of the path (equivalent to path.parents[-1]), and
*parts* is a reversed list of parts following the anchor.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.parent
documentation: The logical parent of the path.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.parents
documentation: A sequence of this path's logical parents.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.is_absolute
documentation:
True if the path is absolute (has both a root and, if applicable,
a drive).

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase._pattern_str
documentation: The path expressed as a string, for use in pattern-matching.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.match
documentation:
Return True if this path matches the given pattern. If the pattern is
relative, matching is done from the right; otherwise, the entire path
is matched. The recursive wildcard '**' is *not* supported by this
method.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PurePathBase.full_match
documentation:
Return True if this path matches the given glob-style pattern. The
pattern is matched against the entire path.

Base class for pure path objects.

This class *does not* provide several magic methods that are defined in
its subclass PurePath. They are: __fspath__, __bytes__, __reduce__,
__hash__, __eq__, __lt__, __le__, __gt__, __ge__. Its initializer and path
joining methods accept only strings, not os.PathLike objects more broadly.

classes.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.stat
documentation:
Return the result of the stat() system call on this path, like
os.stat() does.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.lstat
documentation:
Like stat(), except if the path points to a symlink, the symlink's
status information is returned, rather than its target's.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.exists
documentation:
Whether this path exists.

This method normally follows symlinks; to check whether a symlink exists,
add the argument follow_symlinks=False.
Convenience functions for querying the stat results

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.is_dir
documentation: Whether this path is a directory.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.is_file
documentation:
Whether this path is a regular file (also True for symlinks pointing
to regular files).

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.is_mount
documentation: Check if this path is a mount point

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.is_symlink
documentation: Whether this path is a symbolic link.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.is_junction
documentation: Whether this path is a junction.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.is_block_device
documentation: Whether this path is a block device.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.is_char_device
documentation: Whether this path is a character device.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.is_fifo
documentation: Whether this path is a FIFO.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.is_socket
documentation: Whether this path is a socket.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.samefile
documentation:
Return whether other_path is the same or not as this file
(as returned by os.path.samefile()).

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.open
documentation:
Open the file pointed to by this path and return a file object, as
the built-in open() function does.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.read_bytes
documentation: Open the file in bytes mode, read it, and close the file.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.read_text
documentation: Open the file in text mode, read it, and close the file.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.write_bytes
documentation: Open the file in bytes mode, write to it, and close the file.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.write_text
documentation: Open the file in text mode, write to it, and close the file.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.iterdir
documentation:
Yield path objects of the directory contents.

The children are yielded in arbitrary order, and the
special entries '.' and '..' are not included.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.glob
documentation:
Iterate over this subtree and yield all existing files (of any
kind, including directories) matching the given relative pattern.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.rglob
documentation:
Recursively yield all existing files (of any kind, including
directories) matching the given relative pattern, anywhere in
this subtree.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.walk
documentation: Walk the directory tree from this directory, similar to os.walk().

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.absolute
documentation:
Return an absolute version of this path
No normalization or symlink resolution is performed.

Use resolve() to resolve symlinks and remove '..' segments.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.cwd
documentation: Return a new path pointing to the current working directory.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.expanduser
documentation:
Return a new path with expanded ~ and ~user constructs
(as returned by os.path.expanduser)

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.home
documentation: Return a new path pointing to expanduser('~').

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.readlink
documentation: Return the path to which the symbolic link points.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.resolve
documentation:
Make the path absolute, resolving all symlinks on the way and also
normalizing it.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.symlink_to
documentation:
Make this path a symlink pointing to the target path.
Note the order of arguments (link, target) is the reverse of os.symlink.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.hardlink_to
documentation:
Make this path a hard link pointing to the same file as *target*.

Note the order of arguments (self, target) is the reverse of os.link's.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.touch
documentation: Create this file with the given access mode, if it doesn't exist.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.mkdir
documentation: Create a new directory at this given path.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.rename
documentation:
Rename this path to the target path.

The target path may be absolute or relative. Relative paths are
interpreted relative to the current working directory, *not* the
directory of the Path object.

Returns the new Path instance pointing to the target path.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.replace
documentation:
Rename this path to the target path, overwriting if that path exists.

The target path may be absolute or relative. Relative paths are
interpreted relative to the current working directory, *not* the
directory of the Path object.

Returns the new Path instance pointing to the target path.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.chmod
documentation: Change the permissions of the path, like os.chmod().

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.lchmod
documentation:
Like chmod(), except if the path points to a symlink, the symlink's
permissions are changed, rather than its target's.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.unlink
documentation:
Remove this file or link.
If the path is a directory, use rmdir() instead.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.rmdir
documentation: Remove this directory.  The directory must be empty.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.owner
documentation: Return the login name of the file owner.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.group
documentation: Return the group name of the file gid.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.from_uri
documentation: Return a new path from the given 'file' URI.

methods.name: pathlib_abc.PathBase.as_uri
documentation: Return the path as a URI.

Base class for concrete path objects.

This class provides dummy implementations for many methods that derived
classes can override selectively; the default implementations raise
UnsupportedOperation. The most basic methods, such as stat() and open(),
directly raise UnsupportedOperation; these basic methods are called by
other methods such as is_dir() and read_text().

The Path class derives this class to implement local filesystem paths.
Users may derive their own classes to implement virtual filesystem paths,
such as paths in archive files or on remote storage systems.
